feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Custom CSS is now missing in SAC stories???
entry.content:
I have an existing story that used a lot of custom CSS - today when editing the story the CSS button is missing  & and there's no CSS selector in the styling panel for any of the widgets?

what on earth is going on ? why was this removed? 
A lot of my stories use custom CSS and being unable to edit it etc is a massive problem.

Anyone else seeing this issue?

entry.title: Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to know that if I need to extent days of my Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days then i cant see any option in my tool, it already been nearly to expire now I need for extending, kindly help me and assist how to resolve.

Thanks,

kanchan

entry.title: Seeking Advice on Handling SUMIF-Based Multi- Excel-Sheet Data in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need to build a table in SAC Story. The source data comes from three Excel sheets, and the original Excel table uses SUMIF to consolidate values from these three sheets. I’m wondering if there’s a clean and simple approach for this scenario.

Currently, I created a dedicated helper dimension for display purposes and set up a hierarchy within it. Meanwhile, I added an extra column with IF formulas in the import job to map data to this helper dimension, so the table can be presented correctly in SAC. I’d appreciate it if you could advise whether there is a better practice or alternative method.

I've attached some screenshots for illustration.

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance.

SAP Analytics Cloud, data modeling

entry.title: Unable to Establish OData Connection Between Datasphere and SAC at Root Level
entry.content:
We need to establish a connection with Datasphere as the source and SAC as the target, so that we can import data from any analytical model.

 We can establish individual connections for each analytical model, but it is difficult to maintain multiple connections. We want to avoid creating separate connections for every model. 

is there any workaround : https://me.sap.com/notes/3429304

entry.title: Unable to Add Smart Insights Text to SAC Chart Footer
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I would like to add Smart Insights text as a chart footer according to Help Portal (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/32c43c0fbb004a59bf3ce44444e52b8a.html#add-smart-insights-text-as-a-chart-footer).

However, after clicking on the Smart Insights option for the chart, I can only see the smart insight content in the panel on the right and cannot display the text directly below the chart. In an optimized story, is there a specific setting or option that needs to be enabled to add the Smart Insights text to the chart footer?

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance.

SAP Analytics Cloud, augmented analytics

entry.title: SAC Input Control issue
entry.content:
I have imported the Procurement Dashboard from the Business content. At SAC automatically 3 models got created & were used. 

With One model "SAP__PROC_ANA_LH_POITEMVALUE" the Input controls Company Code, Name of Supplier, Purch. Org. Name, Plant Name shows only "All" option but do not show the individual members. While Material Group Input control shows individual members.

Even if the model mentioned is taken separately in new story and tried to create the 4 input controls, mentioned earlier, shows no member for that dimension & the input control won't get created.

But, when we create a table with these dimensions like Company code, it clearly shows all the members. It means the particular dimensions has the members but at input control those members are unable to find. Below are some screen shots given for the reference.

 

Model is the live model, so we can not make any change in model.

What could be the issue?

entry.title: SAC and Datasphere on the same datacenter
entry.content: I see that SAP has a prerequisite stating that SAC and Datasphere must be on the same data center for Seamless Planning to work. However, in my project, it seems to be working even though they are hosted on different data centers. May I know how it works?

entry.title: How can i annualize data ?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Id like to have a column which result should be ( A_PYG / A_CALC ) * Month/12
Where Month is the selected month on the input control, example April = 4 ; May = 5; etc.

Such as the month formula on Excel. The main reason is to annualize a rate.

entry.title: Data transfer between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Databricks – guidance and issue clarification
entry.content:
Data transfer between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Databricks – guidance and issue clarification. We require guidance on enabling data exchange between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and the Databricks platform.

We specifically need step-by-step guidance and supported approaches for the following:

How to send data from SAC to Databricks and How to send data from Databricks into SAC 

Standard and supported methods for both directions (SAC → Databricks and Databricks → SAC).

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Snowflake - Data Access Agent
entry.content:
Hi

I am trying to activate the Data Access Agent. However, I cannot see the agent as described in the SAC Help. I have granted the necessary permissions and I am also an administrator.
The SAP Analytics Cloud, connectivity SAC is a partner tenant. The version and cloud are the same as for other systems
Does this only work in the SAP Business Data Cloud 

Many thanks and best regards

entry.title: System Requirements SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to know what are the system (hardware and software) requirements to use SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

I found the documentation below, but it is covering software requirements mostly:

https://help.sap.com/doc/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/2023.20/en-US/11b4e5ff76eb4747bc255d7037be1f01.html

However this documentation does not mention hardware requirements, such as minimum processor capacity, memory, disk, etc.

Is there any other SAP documentaion that provides the hardware requirements?

Thank you.

Marcos

entry.title: Connection between SAC Analytics Cloud and SAP HANA Cloud to create a model using model import
entry.content: We are trying to create a model in SAC using the model option, but we are attempting to establish a connection with SAP HANA Cloud, which is in BTP, but this has not been possible. We want to clarify the process to follow, whether it is necessary to use the SAP Cloud Connector and the SAC Agent, or if this type of connection is even possible.

entry.title: 10.000 filtermember limit in Data Analyzer filter by member section (BW LIVE MODEL)
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Data Analyzer, when utilizing an SAP BW live connection, the filter member selection list appears to be hard-capped at 10,000 values. We have been unable to find any official Knowledge Base Articles (KBA) or SAP Notes confirming or explaining this specific UI restriction.

Questions:

  • Is this 10,000-value limitation a hard-coded design restriction in the SAC Data Analyzer UI for BW Live connections?

  • Is there a specific SAP Note, BW profile parameter (e.g., in RSADMIN), or SAC configuration that controls this limit and allows us to increase it?

  • If this is standard product behavior, what is the recommended workaround for business users to search and select members that fall outside the initial 10,000 records if there is a workaround?

Note: There are other ways to filter on the valid from date of 2025. I have informed our business users to utilize the filter by range option.

Steps taken:

We encountered this issue when trying to select filter members greater than 18.05.2017 for the InfoObject Valid from (0DATEFROM) (which references 0DATE).

To investigate, we performed the following deep-dive analysis:

  1. Master Data Verification: In the BW Query Builder, the filter value selection correctly displays all values present in the master data table.

  2. Data Export: We exported the entire master data table for 0DATE (sorted ascending) to Excel (via Eclipse). The table contains a total of 30,982 unique values.

    1. SQL QUERY with max rows set to 100000:

      1. SELECT

        /BI0/SDATE~DATE0,

        /BI0/SDATE~SID,

        /BI0/SDATE~CHCKFL,

        /BI0/SDATE~DATAFL,

        /BI0/SDATE~INCFL

        FROM

        /BI0/SDATE

        WHERE

        DATE0 GE '19900101'

        ORDER BY

        DATE0

  3. The 10k Threshold: In Excel, we added a row counter. The date 18.05.2017 is exactly the 10,000th value in the master data table.

    SAC UI Behavior: In the Data Analyzer "Filter by Member" screen, the system displays exactly 501 pagination tabs:

    • The first member is #(Unassigned).

    • Tabs 1 through 500 each hold exactly 20 members (20 times 500 = 10,000).

    • Tab 501 contains only 1 value: 18.05.2017.

    • Result: The UI cuts off completely after exactly 10,000 master data records, meaning the remaining 20,982 members are entirely missing from the selection list.

entry.title: Clarification on Datasphere Live Connection Scope in SAC
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,
We have created a live connection from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to our SAP Datasphere tenant successfully.
However, we would like to check if it is possible to create a Datasphere live connection in SAC that is restricted to a specific Datasphere space only, instead of the entire tenant.
Our requirement is to use a single Datasphere tenant as multiple environments. For example:

One space is designated for QA/UAT
Another space is used for Performance Testing

We are looking for a way to establish separate SAC live connections for each space to ensure proper isolation between these environments.
Could you please confirm whether this is supported, or suggest the recommended approach to achieve this requirement?

Regards,

China Nageswararao Kalava

entry.title: Clarification on Deprecation of Export Plan Data to S/4HANA (On-Premise)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I have a question regarding the functionality Export Plan Data to SAP S/4HANA from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).
According to the following SAP Help Portal documentation, this functionality is marked as deprecated and will be removed in a future release:
Exporting Plan Data to SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Deprecated) | SAP Help Portal 

At the same time, the new recommended approach described in the following documentation seems to be available only for SAP S/4HANA Cloud edition:
Schedule Financial Plan Data Jobs for SAP Analytics Cloud Integration | SAP Help Portal 

Since we are currently using SAP S/4HANA On-Premise, we would like to understand what is the recommended replacement or successor functionality for exporting plan data from SAC to S/4HANA On-Premise? 

Will there be support for a similar scheduling mechanism for On-Premise systems?

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Kateryna

entry.title: Data Action Function Not Working
entry.content: Data action Function :- CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = {ON | OFF}  is not working as expected . It is not converting Null value to 0 in the code due to which our logic doesnt work.

entry.title: Requirement: Stock Availability vs. No Stock Days for a Product in SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

Business Requirement:
For a user-selected period (for example, 01-May-2026 to 29-May-2026), the business wants to know:

  • Number of days a particular product had stock available.
  • Number of days the product was out of stock (zero or negative stock).

System Landscape:

  • Reporting Tool: SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
  • Data Source: SAP Datasphere
  • CDS  View: I_GOODSMOVEMENTDOCUMENTDEX

Expected Output Example:

Product User input Period Days with Stock Days without Stock

Material A01-May-2026 to 29-May-2026245

24 days the Material available in Stock

5 days Material out of Stock

Any guidance, design recommendations, or implementation examples would be greatly appreciated.

Regards 

SKS

entry.title: SAC Modeler – postingPeriod Not Mapped to Calendar Month for Company Code 2000
entry.content:
In SAP Analytics Cloud, the Balance Sheet report for Company Code 2000 is showing missing/incomplete data. Upon investigation, it has been identified that the source column "postingPeriod" is not mapped to the "Calendar Month" dimension in the Data Foundation (Facts) within the Modeler.

This mapping exists and is working correctly for Company Codes 1000 and 3000, confirming this is isolated to Company Code 2000.
3pl Warehouse location address

entry.title: In SAP Analytics cloud need a report which shows the permissions added in a role
entry.content: In SAP Analytics cloud we got an audit requirement to provide a report which shows the permissions added in all the roles available in the system.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Composite with JS onInitialze not executed if Viewport is configured
entry.content:
I face an issue with stories which contain a composite (using a script) if you call an URL (external call, e.g. using Fiori) together with Viewport configured other than None.

If you call it that way, the Story stops loading data and does not execute the onInitialize script of the composite. Issue also happens if you are in Edit Mode and select View.

Opening the same story from within SAC works.

Current Solution - Setting Viewport Option to None solves that behaviour.

Does anyone have further information or an alternative solution?
